title: Homer Vs Lisa And The 8Th Commandment

jokes.text: Evening, Carver of Graven Images. -Homer the Thief. How's business?
jokes.setup: Biblical characters greeting each other in ancient times
jokes.punchline: They casually identify themselves by their sins/forbidden occupations
jokes.explanation: Clever setup for the episode's theme - sin as casual identity. Strong character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Been a little slow. Not much to steal in the desert.
jokes.punchline: Complains about lack of stealing opportunities in barren desert
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up joke with logical absurdity and deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Do not worry. We'll be wandering out here another two weeks, tops.
jokes.punchline: Casually underestimates the 40-year biblical wandering
jokes.explanation: Brilliant biblical knowledge joke - audience knows it's 40 years, making the optimism hilariously wrong

jokes.text: Zohar the Adulterer. My wife sends her regards. -Yes, she's a good woman. Very good. -Thank you, my lusty friend.
jokes.setup: Adulterer's wife sending regards to another man
jokes.punchline: The obvious implication delivered with oblivious politeness
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed adultery joke with good character consistency

jokes.text: -Moses is back. -Quick! Everybody, look busy.
jokes.punchline: Treating it like avoiding the boss at work
jokes.explanation: Perfect modern workplace dynamic applied to biblical moment. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I will read them in no particular order.
jokes.setup: Moses about to read the sacred Ten Commandments
jokes.punchline: Treats them casually like a grocery list
jokes.explanation: Subverts the gravity of the commandments with casual bureaucratic language

jokes.text: "Thou shalt not make graven images." -Oh, my God!
jokes.punchline: Graven image carver realizes his career is over
jokes.explanation: Perfect character payoff - his reaction is both logical and desperate

jokes.text: "Thou shalt not commit adultery." -Well, looks like the party's over.
jokes.punchline: Casual resignation about his lifestyle ending
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with understated disappointment

jokes.text: Hey, Moses. Keep them coming! "Thou shalt not steal."
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets hit with the stealing commandment
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - Homer's enthusiasm backfires immediately

jokes.text: Sorry, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer just got condemned by commandment
jokes.punchline: Moses' casual apology like he personally inconvenienced him
jokes.explanation: Nice capper - Moses treating divine law like personal preference

jokes.text: That's the most dishonest thing I've ever heard! I should box your ears!
jokes.punchline: His extreme moral outrage over minor cable theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character moment - over-the-top moral indignation

jokes.text: Boy, what's this world coming to? -That's exactly-- -Gotta go.
jokes.setup: Homer appears to agree with Flanders's moral stance
jokes.punchline: Homer cuts off moral discussion to pursue the very thing being condemned
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work. Homer's hypocrisy perfectly executed through structure.

jokes.text: "Myth: Cable piracy is wrong. Fact: Cable companies are faceless corporations which makes it okay."
jokes.punchline: Flawed logic presented as authoritative fact
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate rationalization and moral relativism

jokes.text: It's funny because it's true!
jokes.punchline: Homer's low bar for what constitutes good comedy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's simple humor appreciation

jokes.text: Nothing a month? Yeah, I think we can swing that.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if they can afford cable
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic about free stolen cable being affordable
jokes.explanation: Solid Homer logic joke with good economy

jokes.text: "Myth: It's only fair to pay for quality first-run movies. Fact: Most movies on cable get two stars or less and are repeated ad nauseam."
jokes.punchline: Actually valid criticism of cable programming quality
jokes.explanation: Callback to pamphlet with surprisingly accurate media criticism

jokes.text: We'll teach you to lower your bill by making your own Band-Aids.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and pointless DIY project
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on frugal living show concepts

jokes.text: This is where Jaws eats the boat. Here, Die Hard jumps through the window. This is where Wall Street gets arrested.
jokes.punchline: Reduces complex plots to simple actions, refers to 'Wall Street' as a person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - oversimplifying everything and missing the point

jokes.text: They think people watch that.
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive comment about educational content
jokes.explanation: Good character-consistent dismissal of intellectual content

jokes.text: Live, from New Orleans. This is the World Series of Cockfighting! Son of a gun, we'll have big fun on the bayou tonight.
jokes.punchline: Applied to illegal animal fighting with cheerful song reference
jokes.explanation: Dark humor contrasting violence with cheerful presentation

jokes.text: Oh, pizza.
jokes.setup: Reverend talking about modern conveniences as spiritual distractions
jokes.punchline: Homer gets distracted by exactly what the reverend warned about
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate demonstration of the point being made. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I sat through mercy and forgiveness. Finally, we get to the good stuff.
jokes.punchline: Bart's been bored by positive religious concepts, excited by damnation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - fascination with the morbid and transgressive

jokes.text: Hell is terrible! Maggots are your sheet, worms your blanket.
jokes.punchline: Graphic imagery delivered to young children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about inappropriate religious education for children

jokes.text: -Won't you eventually get used to it? -No. -Yes.
jokes.setup: Child asking logical question about hell
jokes.punchline: Teacher's contradictory answers showing theological confusion
jokes.explanation: Good joke about religious authority being uncertain

jokes.text: -Are there pirates in hell? -Thousands of them. -Oh, baby!
jokes.punchline: Bart gets excited about hell having pirates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - turning damnation into adventure fantasy

jokes.text: Hell, hell, hell, hell....
jokes.setup: Bart's logical justification for saying hell
jokes.punchline: Immediately abuses the justification to say it repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Expected escalation but perfect for Bart's character - pushing boundaries

jokes.text: Hello, I'm Troy McClure. You may remember me from Cry Yuma and Here Comes the Coast Guard!
jokes.punchline: References to obscure, probably terrible productions
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure formula with perfectly awful fake titles

jokes.text: A program-length advertisement!
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazed recognition of what it actually is
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment of dawning realization about media manipulation

jokes.text: -Thank you, Troy. Hi, everybody! -Hi, Dr. Riviera!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic audience response to questionable doctor
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick running gag with perfect audience enthusiasm

jokes.text: It won't cost you a thing. Except your soul!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dramatic moral pronouncement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moral stance with dramatic flair. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Beats the hell out of me!
jokes.punchline: Uses 'hell' casually right after Lisa's moral concerns about souls
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with typical Homer obliviousness to spiritual concerns

jokes.text: Thou shalt not.... Covet... graven images. Something about covet.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember the 8th commandment
jokes.punchline: Completely mangles multiple commandments together
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ignorance - combining multiple commandments incorrectly

jokes.text: Yes. I ate two grapes. Please charge me for them.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for anything else at store
jokes.punchline: Confessing to minor grape theft with extreme guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - extreme moral scrupulousness over tiny infraction

jokes.text: I need a price check on two grapes. You heard me, Phil. Two measly, stinking grapes.
jokes.punchline: Clerk's exaggerated annoyance at pricing two grapes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lisa's moral extremism creating real-world absurdity

jokes.text: Run for the hills, Ma Barker, before I call the feds!
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out Homer didn't pay for breakfast or clothes
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic comparison of family provision to bank robbery
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference with Homer's defensive sarcasm

jokes.text: How can one little insulated wire bring so much happiness?
jokes.punchline: Poetic wonder about simple technology bringing joy
jokes.explanation: Gentle character moment showing Homer's simple appreciation

jokes.text: After the fight, we can watch the still photos on the news.
jokes.setup: Barney suggesting listening to fight on radio
jokes.punchline: Follow-up plan is even more primitive than radio
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of primitive entertainment options

jokes.text: The screen door resting off its hinges. Mangy dog staggering about looking vainly for a place to die.
jokes.punchline: Extremely bleak stereotypes of working-class homes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns class prejudice with darkly poetic imagery

jokes.text: The big fight is one of those rare occasions that I savor the sights, the sounds and the smells of other men.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why he wants to watch at Homer's house
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally suggestive description of enjoying men's company
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns oblivious innuendo with perfect character voice

jokes.text: Well, it was either cable or the mechanical bull. I made my choice and I stand by it.
jokes.setup: Asked why his bar doesn't have cable
jokes.punchline: Bizarre business decision between cable and mechanical bull
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist business logic with perfect Moe stubborn pride

jokes.text: The fight! I'll get my brother, Sanjay, to cover for me. He deplores violence.
jokes.setup: Apu wanting to watch the violent boxing match
jokes.punchline: Gets his pacifist brother to work so he can watch violence
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between brothers' attitudes toward violence

jokes.text: No. It is if he puts anything on it. Jelly, for example.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if stealing bread to feed family is wrong
jokes.punchline: Arbitrarily strict moral distinction about condiments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist religious logic - jelly makes theft immoral

jokes.text: -Should I have him arrested? -That seems like an ideal solution.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what to do about father's cable theft
jokes.punchline: Reverend casually suggests having Homer arrested
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about religious authority suggesting family destruction

jokes.text: You're watching Top Hat Entertainment. Adult programming all day, every day. Except in Florida and Utah.
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary geographical restrictions suggesting different moral standards
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about regional censorship. The specific state choices feel authentic to cable-era restrictions.

jokes.text: -Ay, carumba! -Bart!
jokes.punchline: His shocked catchphrase reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard but effective use of Bart's catchphrase in appropriate context

jokes.text: It's for mommies and daddies who love each other.
jokes.punchline: Sweet euphemism for adult content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge innocent explanation that's technically accurate

jokes.text: Sorry, I'd rather go to heaven.
jokes.setup: Homer offering Lisa to watch horse racing
jokes.punchline: Lisa's moral superiority delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moral stance with childlike directness

jokes.text: There's something wrong with that kid. She's so moral.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating morality as a character flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer value system inversion - morality as problem

jokes.text: I can't afford it. When I can afford to pay for it, I will. But I can't so I'm not going to.
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular non-logic justification
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer circular reasoning but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: It's coming down! The cable stays. The foot has spoken.
jokes.setup: Homer threatening to put his foot down
jokes.punchline: Declares his foot has made the opposite decision
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Homer's authority backfiring on himself

jokes.text: Would you be interested in this stereo? A $500 value. You can have it for $40.
jokes.punchline: Tries to sell stolen goods to the homeowner
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise - burglar as door-to-door salesman

jokes.text: The most beautiful women! Just 50 cents! I'm your host, Bart Simpson! You must be at least 8 years old.
jokes.setup: Bart charging kids to watch adult channel
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously low age requirement for adult content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart entrepreneurial corruption with absurd age limit

jokes.text: -Gross! -Yet strangely compelling.
jokes.punchline: Conflicted reaction capturing adult viewing experience
jokes.explanation: Clever adult observation delivered by children

jokes.text: I wish I was an adult so I could break rules.
jokes.punchline: Thinks adults get to break rules freely
jokes.explanation: Good child's misunderstanding of adult freedom and responsibility

jokes.text: -Did you swipe those from work? -Certainly not. What are you implying?
jokes.setup: Lenny bringing jerkies to fight viewing
jokes.punchline: Defensive response implies he definitely stole them
jokes.explanation: Standard defensive guilt reaction, predictable but functional

jokes.text: -You can't! -I can! You dedicate the fight to your manager! I want to tell you that I'm not watching this fight.
jokes.punchline: Lisa interrupts with her moral protest
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of boxing drama with Lisa's moral stance

jokes.text: Quick! It's Moe. I gotta hide the mugs.
jokes.punchline: Homer hiding his own mugs from his friend
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist paranoia about friend stealing trivial items

jokes.text: Hide the stuff I borrowed from work! -Borrowed? -Okay, that stuff I stole from work. -Oh, okay.
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance once he admits to stealing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing family's casual attitude toward theft.

jokes.text: -Hide the stuff I borrowed from work! -Borrowed? -Okay, that stuff I stole from work.
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects himself to honest term
jokes.explanation: Good quick correction showing Homer's awareness of his dishonesty

jokes.text: Smithers, the Cheetos.
jokes.punchline: Treats junk food like fine cuisine requiring formal service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns class pretension applied to common snack food

jokes.text: It wasn't me. It was my wife's idea. Yeah!
jokes.setup: Cable guys confronting Homer about illegal hookup
jokes.punchline: Immediately throws Marge under the bus
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer cowardice and blame-shifting

jokes.text: Assault. Manslaughter. Stealing cable TV!
jokes.punchline: Cable theft listed alongside violent crimes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback connecting episode theme to boxing narrative

jokes.text: I hate to interrupt your judging me. I've made a couple of important decisions.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their judgment while asserting independence
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment showing rare self-awareness with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Number two: I'm not very fond of any of you.
jokes.punchline: Casually insults all his guests while being righteous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer tactlessness during his moral awakening

jokes.text: Everyone to my place for squishies and microwave burritos.
jokes.setup: Fight ending, guests need new entertainment
jokes.punchline: Apu's convenience store after-party with appropriate snacks
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific party invitation with convenience store items

jokes.text: I once watched Jim Corbett fight an Eskimo bare-knuckled for 113 rounds! Back then, if it was less than 50 rounds, we demanded our nickel back!
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long historical fight with ridiculously low ticket price
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa rambling with absurd historical details and value expectations

jokes.text: Third time's a charm.
jokes.punchline: Takes multiple attempts to cut through cable
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy capping the episode's moral resolution

tmdb_overview: Homer becomes the most popular guy in town when he gets an illegal cable hook-up, but Lisa fears he will go to hell for violating the Eighth Commandment.

guest_stars.name: Pamela Hayden
guest_stars.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Martin Prince / Additional Voices (voice)

insight: Piracy morality play delivers 67 jokes in 22 minutes—solid density masks middling impact scores.
